2:00 P.M. - 7:00 P.M.

3:45 P.M.
City Tour
 Feliz Lusitânia
Architectural complex located in the historic center of Belém. Also known as the “Cidade Velha” (Old City), the site is considered the birthplace of the 
city. Within the complex are some of Belém’s main tourist attractions, such as the Forte do Presépio (Presépio Fort), the Casa das Onze Janelas (House 
of Eleven Windows), the Museu do Círio (Círio Museum), and the Catedral Metropolitana de Belém (Metropolitan Cathedral of Belém). 
(Kindly be advised that no coffee break or meal service will be provided during the City Tour).
 
 Group Photo.
 5:45 p.m. Gather for bus departure.
5:55 p.m. Transfer to Grand Mercure Hotel Belem.
 Dinner on your own
Welcome
to Belém

1:00 p.m. Transfer to Embrapa. 
1:30 P.M.
Embrapa
The Brazilian Agricultural Research Corporation (Embrapa) is the leading 
agricultural in Brazil. Embrapa is one of the largest agricultural research 
corporations of the world, with over half a century of contributions focused 
on innovation, efficiency, and sustainability.
Embrapa has a presence in several countries through its Labex, virtual 
laboratories which facilitate research collaborations and knowledge 
exchange with international partners and offices. There are Labex in the 
United States, France, Netherlands, and the United Kingdom,
South Korea, among other countries.
Opportunities:
Scientific Cooperation: Knowledge exchange sharing good practices and 
studies on scientific and technological research on agriculture.
Technical Cooperation: Capacity to undertake technical cooperation,
Technology Business: Embrapa’s technologies and tech based businesses 
are another opportunity to explore synergies with other countries.
 Presentation of Embrapa’s Activities
 Walkymário de Paulo Lemos, Head of Embrapa Eastern Amazon
 Bruno Giovany de Maria, Deputy Head for Technology Transfer
2:15 p.m. 
 Coffee Break
2:40 p.m. 
 Walking Tour including visits to two Embrapa 
projects:
 Iratama Meliponary
 Socio-Environmental Responsibility Center

 4:00 p.m. Gather for bus departure.
4:10 p.m. Transfer to Grand Mercure Hotel Belem. 
 6:00 p.m. Gather for bus departure from Grand 
Mercure Hotel Belém.
6:10 p.m. Transfer to Casa das Onze Janelas.
6:30 P.M.
Reception at 
Casa das Onze Janelas
A historic colonial mansion and cultural center located in Belém, Pará. 
It offers an opportunity to discover the region’s past, architectural heritage, 
and the vibrant landscape of contemporary Brazilian art. Overlooking
the Guajará Bay, it also provides a unique setting to explore the cultural
intersections of the Amazon—both past and present.
 Networking space with the presence of dignitaries, such as the 
State Governor and Secretaries of State of Pará.
7:00 p.m. 
 Welcome Remarks 
 Representative of the Government of Pará
7:15 p.m. 
 Networking
Networking session with high-level authorities of the State of Pará, 
representatives of the city of Belém, and the ACE Delegation.

9:40 p.m. Transfer to Grand Mercure Hotel Belem. 
Attire: Business - Wear closed-toe shoes at all times. Reminder: All participants must carry a valid passport ID
DAY 1
MONDAY August 25, 2025

DAY 2
 7:20 a.m. Gather for bus departure from Grand 
Mercure Hotel Belém.
7:30 a.m. Transfer to the Federation of Industries of 
the State of Pará (FIEPA).
8:00 A.M.
Federation of Industries of the 
State of Pará (FIEPA) 
With more than 70 years of history, FIEPA is the main representative of Pará’s 
industrial sector, serving as a platform to promote competitiveness, innovation, 
and sustainable development. FIEPA operate several key institutions that 
jointly support industrial growth through education, social development, 
internationalization, and business intelligence. These include:
 National Service for Industrial Learning (SENAI): Provides technical 
education and professional training to support the qualification of the 
industrial workforce.
 Social Service of Industry (SESI): Promotes workers’ quality of life through 
initiatives in health, education, and workplace safety.
 Euvaldo Lodi Institute (IEL): Encourages innovation and entrepreneurship 
by connecting academia and industry.
 International Business Center (CIN): Supports companies in foreign trade 
and global market integration.
 Innovation and Economic Sustainability - REDES: Strengthens local value 
chains through supplier development and sustainable business practices.
 Credit Access Center (NAC): Offers technical guidance and support for 
companies seeking financing.
Collaboration Opportunities:
 FIEPA supports SMEs through its International Business Center (CIN), 
helping them access global markets. 
 REDES strengthens local supply chains and promotes sustainable, 
inclusive business models.
 The NAC program improves credit access through training and
advisory services, while the Industry Observatory provides strategic
insights for industrial development and innovation.
8:00 a.m. - 8:50 a.m. 
 Registration and Breakfast

11:30 a.m. 
 EcoAmazon Fashion Show - Sustainable Runway
Presentation of a fashion show featuring companies in sustainable 
fashion and accessories, inspired by Brazilian biomes. This highlight of 
the event will reinforce Brazil’s cultural richness and creativity, while 
also showcasing the export potential of the sector. The show will 
feature designers from all five macro-regions of Brazil, who will present 
inspiring collections and multiple creative perspectives.
 11:55 a.m. Group Photo.
12:00 p.m. 
 Closing Remarks and Networking brunch with 
FIEPA’s representatives
This event will also honor the 27th anniversary of the FIEPA International 
Business Center
 1:15 p.m. Gather for bus departure.
1:20 p.m. Transfer to Ecopark Benevides. 
2:30 P.M.
Ecopark Benevides
 Ecopark Benevides, established in 2014, holds a series of companies 
with bioeconomy assets at a global scale. The Brazilian company Natura is 
the anchor of the industrial complex that combines innovative companies, 
respect for natural habitats, engagement of the the local community, and 
collaboration among private organizations. 
 Natura &Co Holding S.A. is the largest Brazilian cosmetics company by 
revenue. The Natura & Co Group is present in 73 countries across all continents. 
Natura’s mission combines promoting a beauty experience rooted in the 
delicate balance between nature and science. Their innovative formulas 
combine the biodiversity of the Amazon Forest with the latest, greatest 
cosmetic technology in all their products.
Opportunities:
 Share good practices.
 Innovative nature based solutions.
 Trade and investments opportunities. 
Note: Possible visit to Teatro da Paz afterward, considering it was not 
visited on Sunday. Definition pending on estimated duration of visit to 
Ecopark Benevides.
 5:30 p.m. Group Photo.

DAY 3

8:00 a.m. Transfer to The Federal University of 
Pará (UFPA). 
8:30 A.M.
The Federal University of Pará
The Universidade Federal do Pará (UFPA) is the largest public university in 
the Amazon region with more than 50.000 students and 3.000 faculty 
members across 12 campuses across multiple cities in the state of Pará. 
UFPA is a national reference in research and innovation. Some of its stand 
out fields of study include: biomedical sciences, parasitology, tropical 
diseases and geosciences. UFPA also excels on environmental, social 
science, engineering, computer science and artificial intelligence.
Opportunities:
 Share good practices.
 Knowledge exchange
8:35 a.m. 
 Opening Remarks
 Provost of The Federal University of Pará (UFPA) 
 Provost of The State University of Pará (UEPA) 
8:45 a.m. 
 Presentation of Research conducted by the Local 
Universities.
ACE delegates will have the opportunity to interact and connect with 
projects and initiatives developed by UFPA and UEPA in fields related 
to innovation, environmental preservation and sustainability. This is 
the perfect opportunity to discuss knowledge exchange initiatives, 
partnerships and collaboration agreements with the scientists and 
leaders of the universities of Pará.

Main programs: grants and scholarships for scientific research 
projects; academic and applied research; collaboration between 
universities, research centers, companies; innovation and 
technology transfer; etc.

DAY 4
Breakfast on your own
 8:30 a.m. Gather for bus departure from Grand 
Mercure Hotel Belém.
8:40 a.m. Transfer to Guamá Science and 
Technology Park. 
9:10 A.M.
Guamá Science and 
Technology Park 
The Guamá Science and Technology Park stands out for its activities in 
the areas of Information Technology, Biotechnology, Bioeconomy, Energy, 
and Mining. With more than 30 resident companies, 40 associates, 12 
research and development laboratories, as well as a technical school. This 
collaboration enables a vibrant and dynamic environment that encourages 
applied research, innovative and sustainable entrepreneurship, and the 
generation of solutions for local and global challenges.
Services offered include: 
a) The iConnect network, that brings together independent 
professionals, research and development laboratories and the industrial 
sector; 
b) Export training programm PEIEX that increases the international 
competitiveness of Brazilian companies, and services in innovation 
management, business planning, competitive intelligence and support 
for technology transfer.
Opportunities:
 Share good practices.
 Innovative nature based solutions.
 Trade and investments opportunities. 
9:15 a.m. 
 Welcome Reception
9:45 a.m. 
 Visit to the Guamá Technology Park

12:00 p.m. Transfer to Usina da Paz. 
1:00 P.M.
Usina da Paz 
The “Usina da Paz” are multifunctional complexes with the aim of promoting 
peace, citizenship and social inclusion. These centers have delivered over 
9.7 million citizen services to date including healthcare, education, cultural 
programming, sports, and professional development. All services are offered 
free of charge. Since the first Usina da Paz opened in 2021, more than 14 
units have become fully operational in Pará and other cities.
1:00 p.m. 
 Lunch

Objectives:
Discuss the strategic role of innovation as a driver of economic

DAY 5
Breakfast on your own

Mercure Hotel Belém.
8:00 a.m. Transfer to Dona Nena’s Chocolate 
House on Combu Island.
8:10 A.M.
Casa do Chocolate Da Dona 
Nena on Combu Island
Located on Combu Island in Belém, Casa de Chocolate Dona Nena carries 
out sustainable projects that preserve the forest and honor the ancestral 
knowledge of Amazonian peoples. A successful example of the bioeconomy 
in practice, the company also offers guided tours allowing visitors to 
experience local chocolate production in the heart of the Amazon rainforest.
Opportunities:
 Share good practices.
 Trade opportunities. 
9:30 a.m. 
 Visit to Casa do Chocolate Da Dona Nena on 
Combu Island
Objectives: 
Visit to an example of a business that values environmental 
sustainability and respect for local culture, providing a basis 
for possible reflections on the theme of “sustainable micro-
entrepreneurship”
11:00 a.m. Transfer to Lunch

7:45 P.M.
Gasômetro venue 
Teatro Gasômetro is situated within a botanical park and features a 
distinctive architectural design. The seating area includes a central location 
surrounded by concrete bleachers, framed by openwork iron structures and 
a glass dome that lend the venue an elegant and unique ambiance.
